data.title: Rigetti Computing sells Novera QPU system to Canadian university
data.description:
• Rigetti Computing (RGTI) said on Tuesday that it has sold a Novera QPU system to the University of Saskatchewan.
  • The system was shipped in March, and it will be at the core of the university's first quantum computing system, Rigetti said in a statement. 
  • “By providing hands-on access to real quantum computing hardware, academic institutions like USask are enabling students and researchers to pursue groundbreaking advances in quantum science and technology. We are delighted that USask has selected a Rigetti Novera QPU for their quanTA Centre and congratulate them on this exciting new phase of quantum technology exploration and innovation,” Dr. Subodh Kulkarni, Rigetti CEO, said.

data.title: HMH Holding prices U.S. IPO at $20 per share, to list on Nasdaq
data.description:
• HMH Holding (HMH) on Tuesday said it priced its initial public offering of 10.52 million Class A shares at $20.00 per share.
  • The company granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.578 million shares at the IPO price, less discounts and commissions.
  • Shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on April 1 under the ticker symbol “HMH,” with the offering set to close on April 2, subject to customary conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OpenAI raises $3B from individual investors, closes $123B funding round: report
data.description:
• As part of its mega funding round, OpenAI (OPENAI) has raised more than $3B from individual investors, Bloomberg reported.
  • Some of the funds come from Ark Invest, the news outlet added. 
  • OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar said earlier this month that it had raised an additional $10B from several investors, including Andreessen Horowitz, D.E. Shaw Ventures, MGX, TPG and T. Rowe Price.
  • Last month, OpenAI announced $110N in funding from strategic partners including Amazon (AMZN), Nvidia (NVDA), and SoftBank (SFTBY).
  • Following the funding round, OpenAI is now valued at $852B.

data.title: HUB Cyber Security faces leadership reset as CEO Noah Hershcoviz resigns
data.description:
• HUB Cyber Security (HUBC) announced that CEO Noah Hershcoviz had resigned from his role and the board, effective immediately.
  • Renah Persofsky, the chairperson, will take on more responsibility as the company looks for a new CEO.
  • HUBC shares closed at $0.099, down 17.93% on Tuesday, with after-hours trading up 10.78% to $0.11.

data.title: Iran headlines likely true, but their significance remains questionable – MCC Global’s Michelle Caruso-Cabrera
data.description:
Recent reports suggesting Iran is open to discussions about ending the conflict are likely accurate, but their significance remains highly questionable, according to Michelle Caruso-Cabrera, CEO of MCC Global.

In an interview with CNBC, the foreign affairs specialist cautioned that while the Iranian president and foreign minister have signaled willingness to talk, such diplomatic overtures have historically been tactical delays rather than genuine peace efforts.

“Historically moments like this have been about buying time,” Caruso-Cabrera explained, drawing parallels to past negotiations with Cuba and Venezuela.

She noted that Iran’s strategy often involves seeking ceasefires and extending timelines rather than pursuing meaningful resolutions.

She also pointed out that the U.S. administration appears aware of these delay tactics. She referenced Operation Midnight Hammer, the bombing of Iranian nuclear facilities, as evidence that American officials grew frustrated with what they perceived as Iranian stalling and responded with decisive military action.

Caruso-Cabrera highlighted significant uncertainty about Iran’s internal power structure, noting that the Supreme Leader may be in a coma, potentially leaving the president as the de facto leader.

Complicating matters further, the Iranian Revolutionary Guard made “very contrary statements” the same morning, directly contradicting the diplomatic signals from the president’s faction.

The core U.S. military objective remains destroying Iran’s ability to project power by eliminating its missile capabilities and navy, according to the analyst.

“I don’t think they’re going to stop until they achieve that objective,” Caruso-Cabrera said, suggesting military operations will likely continue regardless of diplomatic headlines.

Despite market optimism surrounding the recent reports, Caruso-Cabrera’s assessment indicates that the path to ending the conflict depends far more on achieving specific military goals than on Iran’s rhetorical willingness to engage in discussions.

More on the war in Iran

  • The recent pullback in stocks has no geopolitical connection, and it is not fundamentally driven – strategist
  • Iran reiterates willingness to end war but needs guarantees- reports
  • Nuclear leverage is most crucial for Iranian regime now – political strategist
  • 3 scenarios to end the war in Iran: a ceasefire, military escalation, or a recession – Rapidan’s McNally

data.title: RH shares crater on missed Q4 results, sets guidance below street view
data.description:
Shares of RH (RH) are on the defensive after the company’s fourth-quarter results and expectations for the first quarter and full year disappointed investors despite indications that the company is navigating “a dire housing market.” This was evidenced by 3.7% revenue growth and more than doubling of unadjusted net income during the final quarter of the year.

The high-end furniture retailer generated $842.6M in revenue (versus $873.25M estimates), contributing to net income of $28.8M, up 107% from the same quarter last year. On an adjusted basis, however, RH (RH) earned net income of $30.1M, down from $31.7M a year ago. On a per-share basis, RH earned a profit of $1.53 per share, down 3.2% year-over-year and less than the consensus estimate of $2.20 per share.  

An operating margin of 11.5% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 17.7% improved from 8.7% and 17.1%, respectively, although missed an operating margin estimate of 12.8%.

Additionally, the company generated $54.6M in free cash flow, compared with negative $70M a year ago.

RH (RH) stated that fourth quarter and 2025 net revenues were negatively impacted by approximately $30M due to higher than anticipated backorder and special order balances as a result of tariff-related resourcing, as well as ~$10M due to adverse weather at the end of the quarter.

For the first quarter, revenue is seen declining by 2% to 4%, translating to a range of $781.4M and $797.7M, below $876.7M estimates. Adjusted EBITDA margin is targeted for 5.5% to 6.5%, compared to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.1% in the same quarter last year. The Q1 estimates include an approximate negative 420 basis point adjusted EBITDA margin impact from pre-opening and startup costs to support the company’s international expansion.

For FY26, revenue is expected to increase by 4% to 8%, representing a range of $3.58B and $3.71B, below the $3.77B consensus estimate. Adjusted EBITDA margin is seen between 14% and 16% versus 17.3% in FY25. This outlook assumes a negative 270 basis point adjusted EBITDA margin impact from pre-opening and startup costs to support international expansion.

Shares are down nearly 20% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Barrick forms executive leadership team to pursue North American IPO - Bloomberg
data.description:
Barrick Mining (B) has formed a new leadership group for its North American operations, as the gold producer advances plans to pursue an IPO for the business, Bloomberg reported Tuesday.

The new management team will oversee Barrick's (B) mines in Nevada and the Dominican Republic and consists of seven positions, according to the report, which also said Barrick executives who currently oversee the North American operations have been named as part of the leadership structure, including Tim Cribb as COO and Wessel Hamman as CFO.

The new structure marks Barrick's (B) latest efforts to separate its North American assets and sell a 10%-15% stake in the entity later this year; the spinoff would include Barrick's interest in the Nevada and Dominican Republic joint ventures with miner Newmont, as well as the company's Fourmile project in Nevada.

A North American spinoff, which could be worth more than $60B, would follow years of declining production and separate the business from operations in more risky jurisdictions such as Mali and Pakistan.

data.title: GFL Environmental near deal for Texas waste operator - report
data.description:
• GFL Environmental (GFL) is close to an agreement to purchase Frontier Waste Solutions, which manages dozens of sites in Texas.
  • A deal would be worth around $900 million, according to a Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited a person familiar with the matter. Some Frontier holders will receive GFL shares as part of the deal. 
  • Dallas-based Frontier was started in 2017 with the purchase of several waste companies in north, south, and central Texas.
  • GFL (GFL) is set to release its Q1 earnings on April 29.

data.title: ‘Oversold technical market conditions look a little bit like the 90s’ – Charles Schwab’s Liz Ann Sonders
data.description:
The stock market (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI) has accumulated significant oversold technical conditions that set the stage for sharp rebounds when positive catalysts emerge, according to Liz Ann Sonders, chief investment strategist at Charles Schwab.

With less than 20% of the S&P 500 (SP500) trading above their 50-day moving average, the market was positioned like a compressed spring before recent gains.

“Sometimes that in and of itself means your sort of launch point,” Sonders said in an interview with CNBC.

Reading investor sentiment has become increasingly complex in the current environment, Sonders explained. The traditional framework of institutional versus individual investors has given way to a more fragmented picture that includes retail traders and requires analyzing both what investors say and what they actually do with their money.

“You’ve got to listen to what they’re saying, but also watch what they’re doing,” she noted, describing the current sentiment landscape as “a real jumbled sentiment picture.”

The strategist emphasized that positioning can shift dramatically in response to headlines and social media posts. When asked about the potential for upside, Sonders framed the key question: “Are we positioned such that if you get a positive catalyst, do you have greater short-term upside by virtue of positioning having gotten washed out?”

Beyond immediate market movements, Sonders warned that the economic ripple effects from current geopolitical tensions extend well beyond oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM).

The supply chain disruptions affect liquefied natural gas (UNG), (BOIL), (UNL), refined products (CRAK), and even helium used in semiconductor (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL) chip production.

“The economic impacts of this, that story is not fully told yet,” she cautioned.

Sonders drew distinctions between current conditions and the 2022 bear market, noting that energy distribution options and export routes were more flexible four years ago.

The 2022 period also featured a massive inflation problem driven by COVID-19 lockdowns under a very different monetary policy backdrop.

Instead, the veteran strategist sees closer parallels to 1990, citing the combination of military conflict, rising oil prices, and underlying credit concerns that have been pushed to the background by war-related headlines.

“To me, this reminds me a little bit of '90,” Sonders said.

data.title: Southern Company ends in the red after six-session rally
data.description:
The Southern Company (SO) snapped six straight sessions of gains on Tuesday as its shares settled 0.42% lower at $96.52.

The company ended in the green in each session between March 23 and March 30. It added more than 3% during the period, compared to a 3% decline in the broader markets.

Earlier this month, the company was upgraded at Evercore ISI as well as KeyBanc.

Evercore analysts  said Southern shares are poised to further break out to new highs, calling it a core utility holding backed by a favorable regulatory backdrop and potential upside from additions to its capital plan.

Meanwhile, KeyBanc's Sophie Karp  argued that the stock is fairly valued, and its Q4 earnings growth outlook revision and constructive news flow should further support the shares.

Overall, Seeking Alpha analysts have a Hold rating for the stock. Seeking Alpha’s quant rating also suggests a Hold recommendation for The Southern Company, with a score of 3.20 out of 5. SO has been rated an A+ for profitability but has been graded a D+ for valuation.

Wall Street analysts are rather bullish on the stock and have issued a Buy rating.

On a year-to-date basis, the stock has inched up by over 10%, while in the past year it rose 4.68%, lagging the wider market.
